exemplar.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained focus on the prescribed title with repeated reference to Boxâs aphorism
Effective use of concrete, discipline-appropriate examples in both Mathematics and Natural Sciences
Clear, coherent arguments in each AOK, including claims, counter-claims and mini-conclusions
Recognition and brief evaluation of different perspectives (utility vs. risk; educational vs. professional contexts)
Ethical awareness demonstrated in the critique of the BMI modelâs real-world consequences
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is uneven: many assertions (e.g. âwrong models accelerate progressâ) are unsupported by evidence or scholarly reference
Underlying knowledge-construction processes (assumptions, validation, paradigm shifts) are not probed in sufficient depth
Introduction fails to pose an explicit knowledge question to frame the essay
Counter-arguments and evaluative comments are often tacked on rather than systematically integrated
Technical inaccuracies (e.g. misrepresentation of the log-normal distribution) and inconsistencies in figure citations disrupt clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although our strand assessment initially placed this essay in the 7â8 band, the official examiner has awarded a total of 9 marks. To reconcile this, we recognize that the student demonstrates a high level of engagement with the title, consistently integrates relevant examples from two AOKs, and shows coherent argumentation with emerging evaluative insight. These qualities justify elevating the overall mark to 9. At the same time, the essayâs analytic depth remains uneven, with key links to knowledge-question frameworks underdeveloped and some technical inaccuracies. Assigning a 9 thus acknowledges the studentâs strong performance in focus, structure and illustrative examples, while still noting the areasâdeeper conceptual analysis, explicit knowledge questioning and technical precisionâwhere improvement is needed.

annotations.comment: The generalisation that wrong models accelerate mathematical development is asserted without evidence or citation. The student should support such claims with specific historical or scholarly references.

The claim that a model âneeds a basis of certaintyâ is not linked to how certainty is established in scientific or mathematical practice. The student should discuss methods of validation and peer review.
The critique of false certainty in Boxâs claim appears late in the essay, which disrupts argument structure. Earlier integration of counter-claims would enhance coherence and balance.
To strengthen the conclusion, the student could propose concrete guidelines for adapting âwrongâ models responsibly, demonstrating how theoretical claims translate into practice.

Listing the chosen areas of knowledge is appropriate, but the essay does not explain why Mathematics and the Natural Sciences are most relevant to the knowledge question. The student should justify the selection of AOKs in relation to epistemic issues.

The opening definitions of mathematical and scientific models are descriptive but lack analytical depth. The student should engage with how these definitions shape the construction and validation of knowledge within each discipline.

The discussion of Boxâs aphorism remains at the level of assertion rather than analysis. The student should interrogate the philosophical underpinnings of âwrong but usefulâ in relation to knowledge production.

Including both the LotkaâVolterra graph and its equations grounds the mathematical example effectively, demonstrating clear discipline-specific representation and linking visual and symbolic forms of knowledge.
Long, complex sentences in this paragraph impede clarity. The student should refine sentence structure to strengthen the coherence of arguments about model usefulness and generalisation.
While the statistics on delayed diagnosis are compelling, the analysis would be deepened by discussing alternative health metrics or model refinements that address BMIâs shortcomings.

The essay highlights versatility through generalisation, but stops short of critically examining trade-offs. A deeper exploration of when oversimplification undermines model transferability is required.
Referencing âFigure 2â for the Brexit example is inconsistent with earlier numbered figures, causing reader confusion. The student should ensure accurate and consistent figure citations.

The prescribed title is clearly stated, but the introduction fails to reformulate it into an explicit knowledge question to guide the essayâs analytical direction. The student should pose a clear knowledge question early to frame and focus the subsequent discussion.

Selecting Pauliâs exclusion principle as a scientific example is a strong choice: it clearly shows how simplified analogies (boxes and arrows) facilitate initial conceptual understanding in the natural sciences.
The essay does not discuss how this model evolves or is superseded by more accurate quantum mechanical formalisms, missing an opportunity to explore the development of scientific knowledge.

The discussion on making knowledge accessible at the expense of accuracy is insightful but could be strengthened by linking explicitly to a knowledge question on the trade-off between simplification and truth.

The ethical critique of the BMI modelâs impact on misdiagnosis successfully connects model limitations to real-world consequences, demonstrating awareness of the moral dimensions of scientific practice.

While the figure is informative, the essay does not critically evaluate how the modelâs oscillatory behaviour reflects its simplifications or limitations relative to Boxâs claim. An explicit analysis of model accuracy versus utility is needed.

The description of the log-normal distribution misrepresents its parameters by focusing on the spread of a logarithm rather than the log of an underlying normal distribution, which undermines technical accuracy.
The discussion of scenario-dependent error tolerance is insightful but needs connection to specific knowledge-question frameworks, such as how context shapes acceptable margins in model-based claims.
The link between oversimplification and practitioner bias is identified, but the student should analyse how scientific communities detect and remediate such biases to improve model reliability.
The table (Figure 3) is referenced but data values are not clearly presented or analysed. Without explicit numerical evidence, the argument about model mismatch lacks empirical support.
The sentence ending with ânature of the scenario thatâ is left incomplete, disrupting coherence. The student must complete and refine this thought to sustain a logical flow.
The causal link drawn between the log-normal modelâs failure and Brexitâs economic impact overlooks confounding variables. A more nuanced economic analysis is needed to justify this assertion.

The conclusion summarises arguments effectively but fails to reflect on implications for the nature of knowledge or re-engage an explicit knowledge question. A stronger conclusion would address these aspects.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
